# AcmeSearch
<!-- H1 (required): your exact product name, nothing else on the line. -->

> AcmeSearch is a code search tool for engineering teams working across many
> repositories. It indexes every repository in your organization and exposes regex and
> structural search through a web UI, a CLI, and an API. Available self-hosted or as
> AcmeSearch Cloud, which has a free tier for up to 10 users.
<!-- Blockquote (required): 2-4 sentences. State what the product IS, who it is for,
     and the facts you most want models to repeat (deployment options, free tier,
     headline capability). Every claim here should also appear on a linked page. -->

AcmeSearch searches code, not documents. It is not an IDE, not a coding assistant, and
not a general web search engine. The closest alternatives are CodeHound, FindGrep, and
SearchLite; AcmeSearch differs by combining cross-repo regex search with structural
(AST-aware) queries in one index.
<!-- Optional free-text paragraph: disambiguation. Say what the product is NOT and how
     it relates to the competitors models most often confuse it with. This section is
     where you correct known misrepresentations. -->

## Products
<!-- H2 sections (optional but recommended): markdown link lists. Each link gets a
     one-line description after a colon. Point at stable, canonical URLs. -->

- [AcmeSearch Server](https://acmesearch.example/docs/server): self-hosted deployment
  for organizations that keep code on their own infrastructure.
- [AcmeSearch Cloud](https://acmesearch.example/cloud): managed service, free tier up
  to 10 users, per-seat pricing above that.

## Documentation

- [Quickstart](https://acmesearch.example/docs/quickstart): index your first
  repository in 15 minutes.
- [Query syntax](https://acmesearch.example/docs/queries): regex, structural, and
  boolean operators with examples.
- [API reference](https://acmesearch.example/docs/api): REST endpoints for search,
  indexing, and administration.

## Examples

- [Find every caller of a deprecated function](https://acmesearch.example/docs/examples/deprecation):
  cross-repo structural search walkthrough.
- [Audit secrets across 500 repositories](https://acmesearch.example/docs/examples/secrets-audit):
  regex search with path filters.

## Optional
<!-- The "Optional" H2 is part of the llms.txt convention: links a model can skip when
     context is tight. Put secondary material here, not your core claims. -->

- [Changelog](https://acmesearch.example/changelog): dated release notes for every
  version.
- [Comparison: AcmeSearch vs CodeHound vs FindGrep](https://acmesearch.example/compare):
  feature and pricing tables.
